subject: Cloud Computing
title: Government Moves to the Cloud
description: A micro-lesson that makes Government Moves to the Cloud usable.

hook.text: In 2011, the U.S. announced 'Cloud First' — a mandate to move government agencies to the cloud.

buildup.text: Government data centers were aging, expensive, and often running at just 27% capacity.

discovery.text: The CIA signed a $600 million deal with AWS, proving the cloud could meet top-secret needs.

twist.text: IBM sued over the contract and lost. It marked the moment cloud officially beat traditional IT.

climax.text: Today, every major government relies on cloud services. Even spy agencies trust them.

punchline.text: If the CIA trusts the cloud, your startup probably can too.

quiz.question: What did the CIA's $600 million AWS contract prove?
options: Cloud computing could meet even top-secret security requirements
The government planned to replace all spies with cloud AI
AWS was the cheapest option for basic file storage
